text.title: event-sourcing-aggregate Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for event-sourcing-aggregate as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Mutating aggregate state directly inside command methods instead of inside apply handlers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents commonly write self.status = "placed" in command methods because it feels natural. This breaks replay: replaying events skips the command path but must reproduce the same state. State mutation belongs in _on_* handlers only.

failure-mode.description: Loading multiple aggregates (Customer, Inventory, Payment) in a single command handler and mutating all of them.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Cross-aggregate mutations are not atomic across event streams. This creates ghost states and eventual consistency violations. Use a saga or process manager to coordinate across aggregates.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without optimistic concurrency, concurrent commands at the same version both succeed. The second write silently overwrites the first, producing incorrect state with no detectable error.
